# tsoa-next documentation for LLMs

This file is a plain-text guide for humans and coding agents implementing APIs with tsoa-next.

Canonical docs
- Home: https://tsoa-next.dev/
- Guides: https://tsoa-next.dev/introduction
- API reference: https://tsoa-next.dev/reference/
- GitHub: https://github.com/tsoa-next/tsoa-next

Project overview
- tsoa-next continues the original tsoa project.
- It generates OpenAPI specifications and runtime route handlers from TypeScript controllers and models.
- Supported runtime targets include Express, Koa, and Hapi. Custom route templates are also supported.
- Current support policy in the docs is Node.js 22 or newer and npm 10 or newer.

Core ideas
- TypeScript controllers and models are the source of truth.
- OpenAPI metadata is generated from TypeScript types and decorators.
- Runtime request validation is handled by generated routes.
- JSDoc is used for descriptive text where types are not enough.
- OpenAPI 3 reduces schema-expressiveness gaps compared with OpenAPI 2.

Quick start checklist
1. Install runtime dependencies such as `tsoa-next`, your web framework, and `reflect-metadata` when your setup needs it.
2. Install TypeScript and framework type packages.
3. Create `tsoa.json` with at least:
   - `entryFile`
   - `controllerPathGlobs`
   - `spec.outputDirectory`
   - `spec.specVersion`
   - `routes.routesDir`
4. Enable `experimentalDecorators` in `tsconfig.json`.
5. Use named exports for controllers. Default-exported controllers are not supported.
6. Write controllers with `@Route`, HTTP method decorators, and parameter decorators.
7. Generate files with `tsoa spec` and `tsoa routes`, or use the programmatic APIs.
8. Import generated `RegisterRoutes` into the app, after body parsers and before final error handling.

Important config notes
- `noImplicitAdditionalProperties` controls excess property behavior:
  - `ignore`
  - `silently-remove-extras`
  - `throw-on-extras`
- `spec.specVersion` can be `2` or `3`.
- `routes.authenticationModule` is used for auth middleware integration.
- Generated routes import from `tsoa-next`, so controllers and generated routes use the same package surface.
- `emitDecoratorMetadata` is not required for basic tsoa-next usage. The docs only call it out as relevant for some custom middleware patterns.

Controller authoring rules
- Decorate classes with `@Route('path')`.
- Decorate methods with `@Get`, `@Post`, `@Put`, `@Patch`, `@Delete`, and related decorators.
- Use parameter decorators to bind request data:
  - `@Path`
  - `@Query`
  - `@Queries`
  - `@Header`
  - `@Body`
  - `@BodyProp`
  - `@FormField`
  - `@UploadedFile`
  - `@UploadedFiles`
  - `@Request`
  - `@Res`
- Path parameters follow OpenAPI path templating, for example `@Get('{userId}')`.

Generation commands
- CLI:
  - `tsoa spec`
  - `tsoa routes`
- Programmatic:
  - import from `tsoa-next/cli`
  - `generateSpec(...)`
  - `generateRoutes(...)`

Runtime setup pattern
- Add framework body parsers first.
- Call `RegisterRoutes(app)`.
- Add a 404 handler if desired.
- Add a global error handler last.

Validation and error handling
- Generated routes validate request input before controller execution.
- Catch `ValidateError` in your framework error middleware and return a structured 422 response.
- Document known error responses with `@Response<...>(422, 'Validation Failed')` or similar.
- `@Res()` can inject typed alternate responders for non-success responses.

External validators
- The external schema decorator is named `@Validate(...)`.
- If someone asks for `@Validator`, they usually mean `@Validate`.
- Supported forms:
  - `@Validate(schema)`
  - `@Validate('zod', schema)`
  - `@Validate({ kind: 'zod', schema })`
- Supported libraries:
  - `zod`
  - `joi`
  - `yup`
  - `superstruct`
  - `io-ts`
- Supported parameter decorators for external validation:
  - `@Body`
  - `@BodyProp`
  - `@Query`
  - `@Queries`
  - `@Path`
  - `@Header`
  - `@FormField`
  - `@UploadedFile`
  - `@UploadedFiles`
- Behavior:
  - TypeScript types still generate OpenAPI.
  - The external schema replaces built-in runtime validation for the decorated parameter subtree.
  - Routes without `@Validate(...)` keep existing behavior.
- Practical guidance:
  - Keep TypeScript types and external schemas aligned.
  - Import controller modules before route registration so decorator metadata exists at runtime.
  - If you maintain custom route templates, preserve the validation metadata plumbing for controller class, method name, and parameter index.
- Library-specific installation:
  - `npm install zod`
  - `npm install joi`
  - `npm install yup`
  - `npm install superstruct`
  - `npm install io-ts fp-ts io-ts-types`
- External validator guide:
  - https://tsoa-next.dev/external-validators

Authentication
- Define security schemes in the OpenAPI config.
- Point `routes.authenticationModule` at a module exporting framework-specific authentication handlers.
- Use `@Security('name', ['scope'])` on controller methods.
- The security definition name in config must match the decorator security name.
- Guide:
  - https://tsoa-next.dev/authentication

File uploads
- Install `multer` and `@types/multer` for Express-based file upload workflows.
- Use `@UploadedFile`, `@UploadedFiles`, and `@FormField` for multipart inputs.
- Custom multer handling can be done inside controller methods when you need custom storage behavior.
- Guide:
  - https://tsoa-next.dev/file-upload

Custom middleware and custom validation
- `@Middlewares(...)` can apply framework middleware to endpoints.
- The existing custom validation guide documents a middleware-based `class-validator` approach.
- That guide is separate from `@Validate(...)`, which is the built-in external-schema integration.
- Guides:
  - https://tsoa-next.dev/custom-middlewares
  - https://tsoa-next.dev/custom-validation
  - https://tsoa-next.dev/external-validators

Descriptions and annotations
- Use JSDoc for endpoint and model descriptions.
- Use annotations and decorators when types alone are not enough.
- Guides:
  - https://tsoa-next.dev/descriptions
  - https://tsoa-next.dev/annotations
  - https://tsoa-next.dev/decorators

Decorators commonly referenced in the docs
- `@Route`
- `@Tags`
- `@Security`
- `@OperationId`
- `@Deprecated`
- `@Hidden`
- `@Request`
- `@Validate`
- Decorator guide:
  - https://tsoa-next.dev/decorators

Dependency injection
- tsoa-next supports IOC container integration.
- The docs include examples using runtime IOC interfaces and controller resolution patterns.
- Guide:
  - https://tsoa-next.dev/di

Templates and custom frameworks
- Express, Koa, and Hapi route generation are built in.
- Custom Handlebars route templates can support other frameworks.
- Guides:
  - https://tsoa-next.dev/templates
  - https://tsoa-next.dev/routes

Path mapping and project structure
- The docs include guidance for TypeScript path mapping and how it interacts with generation.
- Guide:
  - https://tsoa-next.dev/path-mapping

Examples and migrations
- Examples guide:
  - https://tsoa-next.dev/examples
- Upgrading guide:
  - https://tsoa-next.dev/upgrading

Guide map
- Introduction: https://tsoa-next.dev/introduction
- Getting started: https://tsoa-next.dev/getting-started
- Generating routes and OAS: https://tsoa-next.dev/generating
- Error handling: https://tsoa-next.dev/error-handling
- Descriptions: https://tsoa-next.dev/descriptions
- Examples: https://tsoa-next.dev/examples
- Annotations: https://tsoa-next.dev/annotations
- Custom middlewares: https://tsoa-next.dev/custom-middlewares
- Custom validation with class-validator: https://tsoa-next.dev/custom-validation
- External validators with @Validate: https://tsoa-next.dev/external-validators
- Dependency injection: https://tsoa-next.dev/di
- Authentication: https://tsoa-next.dev/authentication
- Decorators: https://tsoa-next.dev/decorators
- FAQ: https://tsoa-next.dev/faq
- File upload: https://tsoa-next.dev/file-upload
- Path mapping: https://tsoa-next.dev/path-mapping
- Templates: https://tsoa-next.dev/templates
- Routes: https://tsoa-next.dev/routes
- Upgrading: https://tsoa-next.dev/upgrading
- API reference: https://tsoa-next.dev/reference/

When in doubt
- Prefer the guides for workflow and patterns.
- Prefer the API reference for exact type signatures and config shapes.
- Keep generated OpenAPI, controller types, and runtime validation rules aligned.
